title: Gitee git auth (PAT) + host-keyed runGit credential injector
prompt:
Add Kanban-hosted Gitee git auth, and generalize the runGit credential injector to be host-keyed. Design + rationale: vault decision cf0d6. Mirror the existing GitHub auth (src/github-auth/) where sensible, but follow the decision's two deviations.

KEY CONSTRAINT — Gitee has NO OAuth device flow. Do NOT build a device-flow/OAuth login for Gitee. Use a pasted Personal Access Token (PAT).

Part 1 — Gitee auth module (src/gitee-auth/):
- Mirror src/github-auth/ MINUS device-flow + pending-login machinery (no github-device-flow.ts / github-pending-login-store.ts equivalent, no OAuth client id).
- Store the PAT machine-local at ~/.kanban/settings/gitee-auth.json (0600), same convention/permissions as github-auth.json. Override env var like KANBAN_GITEE_AUTH_FILE. NEVER write to <repo>/.kanban, never commit, never log the token.
- Capture the Gitee username alongside the PAT (HTTPS basic-auth pair = username:PAT). Verify whether Gitee accepts a fixed username (e.g. 'oauth2') with the PAT as password; if it does, the username field can be optional — but default to capturing it to guarantee auth works. Note findings in the task summary.
- Service surface mirroring GitHubAuthService: setToken/login(accept PAT), getStatus, logout, and getGitInjection() returning the gitee.com-scoped credential {args, env}. mtime-cache the disk read like the GitHub service so a CLI write is picked up by a long-lived runtime.
- Credential injection scoped to https://gitee.com via the same per-URL credential-helper mechanism as github-git-credentials.ts (credential.https://gitee.com.helper resets + installs; token only in per-spawn env; GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0). SSH and all other hosts untouched.

Part 2 — Generalize the runGit injector seam (host-keyed):
- Today src/workspace/git-utils.ts has a single setGitHubGitAuthInjector + gitHubGitAuthInjector. Refactor to a HOST-KEYED registry: runGit asks every registered per-host credential source for its {args, env} and merges them onto the invocation (multiple per-URL helpers coexist cleanly). Keep git-utils.ts a LEAF module (no import cycle) — same setter-registration pattern as today.
- startServer() (cli.ts) registers BOTH sources: the existing GitHub injection (() => getGitHubAuthService().getGitInjection()) and the new Gitee one (() => getGiteeAuthService().getGitInjection()). A throwing/failing injector degrades to no injection (must never break the git op) — preserve today's behavior per source.
- Preserve exact current behavior when logged out of a host: zero injected args/env for that host.

Part 3 — surfaces:
- CLI: 'kanban gitee login' (accept a PAT — flag or stdin prompt, NOT a device-flow run), 'kanban gitee logout', 'kanban gitee status'. Token must NEVER appear in --json output (mirror passcode handling).
- Settings UI (web-ui): a Gitee card alongside the existing GitHub git auth card — paste PAT + username, show status (logged-in account), clear/logout. Reuse the GitHub card's patterns/hook shape (use-github-auth) where it makes sense. Follow web-ui conventions (Tailwind v4 tokens, Radix, lucide, ui/ primitives, sonner, web-ui logger not console.*).
- Add a tRPC 'gitee' router mirroring the relevant subset of the 'github' router (status/setToken/logout — no begin/poll login).

Tests: mirror test/runtime/github-auth/ for the gitee store + credential-injection helpers, and add a test for the host-keyed injector merging multiple sources. Keep the github-auth tests green (the seam refactor must not regress GitHub injection).
